description: Figure 10 presents fractographic and cross-sectional microstructural analysis of failed tensile specimens from Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) low-density steels after hot rolling (HR), cold rolling (CR), and heat treatment at 1023 K. Panels (a–c) show SEM fractographs of (a) 28Mn0Ni, (b) 28Mn5Ni, and (c) 15Mn5Ni alloys. Both 28Mn0Ni and 28Mn5Ni exhibit dimple morphology characteristic of ductile fracture, whereas 15Mn5Ni displays a distinctive quasi-cleavage surface with numerous microcracks, indicating crack-initiation-controlled brittle failure. Panels (d–f) examine the cross-section of the 15Mn5Ni failed specimen: (d) an SEM image revealing intergranular cracks, (e) an OIM-IPF orientation map, and (f) a phase contrast map showing FCC (red) and B2 (blue) distributions. The EBSD data demonstrate that crack propagation follows γ/B2 interphase boundaries. Together, these results reveal that reduced Mn and added Ni in 15Mn5Ni suppress austenite recrystallization and promote extensive B2 precipitation along grain boundaries, causing intergranular and interphase brittle fracture that explains its markedly inferior tensile ductility relative to the higher-Mn alloys.
